name: Proto-Sino-Tibetan (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Proto-Sino-Tibetan is the reconstructed common ancestor of the Sino-Tibetan (Trans-Himalayan) family, including Sinitic, Bodish (Tibetan) and Lolo-Burmese (Burmese). It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Matisoff, J. A.
sources.title: Handbook of Proto-Tibeto-Burman
sources.notes: Reference for Sino-Tibetan reconstruction (structural node only).
